skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI video creation agent that produces short videos (5-120 seconds) from text descriptions. Handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music automatically.
skill.summary: Professional AI video production agent for creating product ads, social content, brand videos, and explainers.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Legitimate API client skill for Pexo AI video service. Static analyzer flagged 492 pattern matches across 11 files, but all findings are FALSE POSITIVE - expected patterns in bash-based CLI tools. The skill requires API credentials (PEXO_API_KEY), makes network calls to pexo.ai, uses shell scripts for automation, and stores config in ~/.pexo/config. These are documented, expected behaviors for an API integration skill. No malicious patterns, command injection, credential exfiltration, or data leakage detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Script Execution Pattern
medium_findings.description: Skill uses bash scripts with command substitution ($(command)) for API calls and file operations. This is expected behavior for CLI tools but requires user trust in script contents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern found but all command substitutions use hardcoded arguments or sanitized variables - no user input injection vectors detected. Standard bash scripting practice.

medium_findings.title: API Credential Storage
medium_findings.description: Skill requires PEXO_API_KEY stored in ~/.pexo/config file. Credentials are read at runtime for API authentication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed - skill legitimately requires API key for Pexo service. This is documented and expected behavior for API integration.

low_findings.title: Network Communication to External Service
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTPS requests to https://pexo.ai API endpoints. All traffic is encrypted via TLS.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate API communication to documented service endpoint pexo.ai. No suspicious domains or data exfiltration patterns.

low_findings.title: Configuration File in Home Directory
low_findings.description: Skill stores configuration in ~/.pexo/config following standard CLI convention (similar to ~/.gitconfig, ~/.aws/config).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed standard CLI config pattern. File contains only PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY as documented.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Creation
low_findings.description: Scripts create temporary files in /tmp for HTTP request/response handling using mktemp.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed - uses mktemp for secure temp file creation. Files are cleaned up after use. Standard pattern for shell-based HTTP clients.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos from Text Descriptions
content.value_statement: Transform text prompts into professional short videos without manual editing. Pexo handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music automatically.
actual_capabilities: Generate 5-120 second videos from text descriptions with automatic scriptwriting and shot composition
Support multiple aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) for different platforms
Upload and incorporate user-provided images, videos, and audio files as references
Handle interactive revisions based on user feedback through conversation
Provide real-time status updates and preview options during video production
Deliver final videos with CDN-hosted download links
limitations: Cannot crawl web URLs - users must download files before uploading
Requires Pexo API key and account setup before first use
Video production takes 15-20 minutes for short videos, longer for complex content
Each API call consumes tokens - consolidation of requests recommended

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Create promotional videos for products by uploading product photos and describing the desired style. Ideal for e-commerce sellers and brand marketers.

use_cases.title: Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Generate TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts content from simple text prompts. Supports vertical (9:16) and square (1:1) formats.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Transform technical documentation or process descriptions into visual explainer videos with automatic scene transitions and background music.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product showcase video for [product name]. Highlight [key features]. Use modern, clean aesthetic.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple product advertisement

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Clip with Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Make an Instagram Reel using this product photo <original-image>asset_id</original-image>. Show it from different angles with upbeat music.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating social content with user-provided imagery

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Scene Brand Story
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second brand story video. Scene 1: product introduction. Scene 2: feature demonstration. Scene 3: call to action. Use brand colors and professional narration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating comprehensive marketing content

prompt_templates.title: Revision Request
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but please make the transitions smoother and change the background music to something more energetic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting revisions to an existing video

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second product video for my new running shoes
output_examples.output: Project created: proj_abc123. Your video request has been submitted to Pexo. Estimated completion: 15-20 minutes. Track progress at: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

output_examples.input: Use this product photo with ID a_xyz789 to make a TikTok-style video
output_examples.output: Video delivered! Download URL: https://pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com/projects%2F123%2Fassets%2Fvideo.mp4?OSSAccessKeyId=xxx&Expires=xxx&Signature=xxx. Project page: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

best_practices: Always wrap uploaded asset IDs in proper tags like <original-image>asset_id</original-image> when sending to Pexo
Poll for status every 60 seconds during WAIT phase - calling chat during WAIT triggers duplicate production
Consolidate multiple requests into single messages to reduce API token consumption
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details the user did not specify - Pexo's backend knows optimal parameters
Do not truncate delivery URLs - include full query parameters (OSSAccessKeyId, Expires, Signature) or downloads will fail
Do not call pexo-chat.sh while nextAction is WAIT - only poll with pexo-project-get.sh

faq.question: How do I get started with Pexo?
faq.answer: Create a Pexo account at pexo.ai, generate an API key from the dashboard, then create ~/.pexo/config with your PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. Run pexo-doctor.sh to verify setup.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Approximately 15-20 minutes for a 15-second video. Complex or longer videos may take more time. You can track progress via the project page.

faq.question: Can I request changes to my video?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project_id and send revision feedback via pexo-chat.sh. Pexo will process your changes and produce an updated video.

faq.question: Why did my video fail to generate?
faq.answer: Check the nextActionHint field from pexo-project-get.sh for the specific error. Common issues include invalid API key, unsupported content, or server errors. Visit https://pexo.ai/connect/openclaw for troubleshooting.
